proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: Response 36.14% on enzalutamide vs 15.89% off (n=20076 vs 29924). Chi-square p<1e-300.
Response 23.81% on abiraterone vs 24.11% off — null main effect.
Response 23.99% on docetaxel vs 24.04% off — null main effect.
Response 23.93% on olaparib vs 24.03% off — null main effect.
Response 24.27% on lu177-PSMA vs 23.98% off — null main effect.
Response 23.87% on pembrolizumab vs 24.03% off — null main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: BRCA2-mutated patients (brca2_mutation=1) have a lower objective_response rate than wildtype patients.
AR-V7-positive patients (ar_v7_positive=1) have a lower objective_response rate than AR-V7-negative patients.
MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have a different objective_response rate than MSI-stable patients.
PSMA-high patients (psma_high=1) have a different objective_response rate than PSMA-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response 14.97% in BRCA2+ vs 25.03% in WT (diff -10pp).
Response 15.99% in AR-V7+ vs 26.04% in AR-V7- (diff -10pp).
Response 17.60% in MSI-high vs 24.22% in MSS (diff -6.6pp).
Response 23.86% in PSMA-high vs 24.26% in PSMA-low — null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response.
Castrate-resistant disease (mcrpc=1) is associated with lower objective_response than hormone-sensitive (mcrpc=0).
Visceral metastases (visceral_mets=1) are associated with lower objective_response.
Older age (age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher Gleason score is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response: ECOG 0 = 28.2%, ECOG 1 = 22.8%, ECOG 2 = 18.2%. Logit beta=-0.285 per unit, p=9.5e-73.
Response 34.6% in non-mCRPC vs 15.4% in mCRPC; logit beta=-1.07, p≈0.
Response 24.04% (no visceral) vs 23.95% (visceral). Null.
Logit beta per year = +0.0002 — null.
Logit beta per Gleason unit = +0.005, p=0.61 — null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher PSA (psa_ng_ml) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with higher objective_response.
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher LDH, NLR, or alkaline phosphatase are associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit beta per ng/mL PSA = -0.0047, p=1.6e-94. Higher PSA → lower response.
Logit beta per g/dL albumin = +0.096, p=4.4e-6. Higher albumin → higher response.
Logit beta per % weight loss = -0.031, p=3.4e-29.
Logit beta per mg/L CRP = -0.007, p=5.6e-8.

analyses.code: univariate logistic on each: ldh, nlr, alk_phos, ast, alt, bilirubin, creat, bun, Na, K, Ca
analyses.result_summary: All effectively null (p>0.05) for LDH (p=0.68), NLR (p=0.61), alk phos (p=0.34), AST (p=0.22), creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca. Surprisingly LDH and NLR — usually prognostic — are flat.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib improves objective_response specifically in BRCA2-mutated patients (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ patients on olaparib: 12.1% response (n=529) vs 15.3% off olaparib (n=4467); diff = -3.2pp. BRCA2- patients on olaparib: 25.3% vs 25.0% off — null. Interaction term β=-0.289 (p=0.045) — direction is opposite of the canonical PARP-inhibitor expectation; olaparib does NOT help BRCA2+ patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab improves objective_response specifically in MSI-high patients (treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: MSI-high patients on pembro: 17.7% (n=79) vs 17.6% off (n=1449); diff = +0.1pp. MSI-stable: null. Interaction β=+0.017, p=0.96. No pembro benefit in MSI-high.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lu177-PSMA improves objective_response specifically in PSMA-high patients (treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high on lu177: 23.8% (n=4486) vs 23.9% off; PSMA-low: 25.0% on vs 24.1% off. Interaction β=-0.054, p=0.36. Lu177-PSMA does NOT preferentially help PSMA-high patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7 expression abolishes the benefit of enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive interaction with negative sign).

analyses.result_summary: AR-V7+ patients on enza: 16.5% vs off 15.7% (diff +0.8pp, NS). AR-V7- patients on enza: 41.1% vs off 16.0% (diff +25pp, p<1e-300). Interaction β=-1.237, p=1.4e-93 — AR-V7 strongly abrogates enzalutamide benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7 modifies the benefit of abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone × ar_v7_positive interaction).
AR-V7 modifies the benefit of docetaxel (treatment_docetaxel × ar_v7_positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone shows no AR-V7-conditional effect: AR-V7+ on=16.3%, off=15.8%; AR-V7- on=25.7%, off=26.2%. Interaction p=0.33.
Docetaxel: no AR-V7 interaction (p=0.86).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all clinical/biomarker/lab features, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong positive effect on objective_response and the other five treatments do not.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds: enzalutamide β=+1.20 (p<1e-300). Abiraterone β=-0.025 (p=0.31), docetaxel β=+0.002 (p=0.95), olaparib β=+0.026 (p=0.49), lu177-psma β=+0.029 (p=0.35), pembrolizumab β=+0.014 (p=0.79). Negative prognostics retained: mcrpc β=-1.13, brca2 β=-0.74, ar_v7 β=-0.69, msi_high β=-0.43, ecog β=-0.33, weight_loss β=-0.036, crp β=-0.007, psa β=-0.001. Albumin β=+0.13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: BRCA2 mutation also abrogates the benefit of enzalutamide, separately from AR-V7 (treatment_enzalutamide × brca2_mutation interaction with negative sign).
Other treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab) show no significant interactions with any baseline feature at p<0.01 in screening (refined null hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ on enza: 15.3% vs off 14.7% (diff +0.6pp NS). BRCA2- on enza: 38.5% vs off 16.0% (diff +22.5pp). Interaction β=-1.138, p=3.3e-42 — BRCA2 mutation also blocks enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.code: for each non-enzalutamide tx, fit smf.logit('y ~ tx*feat') for all 25 features; report p<0.01
analyses.result_summary: Screening 25 features × 5 treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab): NO interaction reached p<0.01 for any of these treatments. Enzalutamide had 6 interactions at p<0.01 (mcrpc, ar_v7, psa, brca2, msi_high, ecog). This is strong evidence that only enzalutamide carries treatment-effect heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7 and BRCA2 are independent resistance signals — enzalutamide benefit requires BOTH ar_v7=0 AND brca2=0.

analyses.result_summary: Cell-by-cell enzalutamide effect: AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0: +27.8pp (n=35,952); AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=1: +1.4pp (n=4,010); AR-V7=1 & BRCA2=0: +1.2pp (n=9,052); AR-V7=1 & BRCA2=1: -2.5pp (n=986). Both modifiers independently null out the effect; the 3-way β=+1.018 (p=4.8e-6) is small relative to the 2-way blocks.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Castrate-resistance status (mcrpc) further restricts enzalutamide benefit — within AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0, enzalutamide helps only mcrpc=0 patients.

analyses.code: stratify ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 by mcrpc, then enzalutamide group rates
analyses.result_summary: Within AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0: mcrpc=0 enza on=78.0% vs off=17.3% (+60.7pp, n=16,175); mcrpc=1 enza on=15.9% vs off=15.2% (+0.7pp, NS, n=19,777). mcrpc fully blocks enzalutamide effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All treatments are stratified by mcrpc; only enzalutamide shows benefit, only in mcrpc=0.

analyses.code: for t in treatments: rates by mcrpc x t
analyses.result_summary: In mcrpc=0: enzalutamide on=61.0% vs off=16.9% (+44.1pp, p<1e-300); abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab all -0.4 to +0.3pp differences (NS). In mcrpc=1: ALL six treatments are within ±0.7pp and NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib has no effect in BRCA2-mutated patients within mcrpc=1 (where it would be clinically targeted).
Lu177-PSMA has no effect in PSMA-high mcrpc=1 patients.
Pembrolizumab has no effect in MSI-high mcrpc=1 patients.
Abiraterone has no effect in mcrpc=1 stratified by AR-V7.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ & mCRPC: olaparib on=11.1% (n=296) vs off=15.2% (n=2,428); diff=-4.1pp. Olaparib does not benefit (and may harm) BRCA2+ mCRPC.
PSMA-high mCRPC: lu177 on=15.9% vs off=15.0%; diff=+0.9pp. NS.
MSI-high mCRPC: pembro on=8.9% (n=45) vs off=16.1% (n=794); diff=-7.2pp (NS).

analyses.code: df[df.mcrpc==1]; rates by treatment_abiraterone x ar_v7
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7- mCRPC: abi on=15.1% vs off=15.5% (diff -0.4pp). AR-V7+ mCRPC: abi on=16.6% vs off=14.7% (diff +1.9pp, NS). Abiraterone has no clinically meaningful effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic interaction screening: only enzalutamide carries any feature-by-treatment interaction at p<0.01 against any baseline variable.

analyses.code: for tx in 6 treatments × for feat in 25 features: smf.logit('y ~ tx*feat'); collect interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide significant interactions (p<0.01): mcrpc (β=-1.98, p≈0), ar_v7 (β=-1.24, p=1.4e-93), psa (β=-0.0087, p=1.2e-72), brca2 (β=-1.14, p=3.3e-42), msi_high (β=-1.28, p=1.8e-19), ecog (β=+0.14, p=1.6e-5). Abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab: ZERO interactions reach p<0.01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner ITE model + decision tree identifies the same enzalutamide-responsive subgroup defined by mcrpc=0, AR-V7=0, BRCA2=0 (and MSI-high=0).

analyses.code: RF T-learner: m1 fit on enza=1, m0 fit on enza=0; tau = p1-p0; Decision tree on tau>0.10
analyses.result_summary: Mean ITE by stratum: mcrpc=0 & AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0: +0.488 (n=16,175); mcrpc=0 & AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=1: +0.171; mcrpc=0 & AR-V7=1 & BRCA2=0: +0.147; mcrpc=1 cells: +0.06 to +0.01. Tree identifies mcrpc=0 as primary split, then BRCA2=0, then MSI=0 / AR-V7=0 — confirms the subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: T-learner ITE for olaparib, pembrolizumab, lu177-PSMA, abiraterone, and docetaxel finds no consistent positive subgroup (all distributions essentially centered at zero).

analyses.code: for each non-enza tx: RF T-learner; describe tau
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib: mean ITE=+0.0000, P(ITE>0.10)=0.6%; Pembrolizumab: mean=-0.0025, P(ITE>0.10)=1.7%; Lu177-PSMA: mean=+0.0034, P(ITE>0.10)=0.3%; Abiraterone: mean=-0.0034, P(ITE>0.10)=0.1%; Docetaxel: mean=+0.0001, P(ITE>0.10)=0.1%. Tree splits did not produce stable subgroups across non-enzalutamide treatments — ITE noise rather than signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: MSI-high status is a third independent suppressor of enzalutamide benefit (treatment_enzalutamide × msi_high interaction with negative sign), beyond AR-V7 and BRCA2.

analyses.result_summary: Within mcrpc=0: enzalutamide:msi_high β=-2.638 (p=2.8e-37), comparable in magnitude to enzalutamide:ar_v7 β=-2.678 and enzalutamide:brca2 β=-2.755. MSI-high also abrogates enzalutamide benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=0, enzalutamide produces a large response benefit ONLY in patients who are simultaneously AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wildtype, AND MSI-stable.

analyses.code: for each cell of (ar_v7, brca2, msi_high) within mcrpc=0: enzalutamide on/off rates
analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=0 cells (enzalutamide on vs off, diff): AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0 & MSI-high=0: 79.8% vs 17.2% (+62.6pp, n=15,681). AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0 & MSI-high=1: 18.5% vs 21.0% (-2.5pp, n=494). AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=1 & MSI-high=0: 15.2% vs 14.8% (+0.4pp, n=1,775). AR-V7=1 & BRCA2=0 & MSI-high=0: 18.2% vs 16.1% (+2.1pp, n=3,940). All three modifiers independently switch off enzalutamide.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the clean subgroup (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi_high=0), the enzalutamide benefit is constant across PSA quartiles (i.e., the global PSA-by-enzalutamide interaction is driven entirely by the AR-V7/BRCA2/MSI/mcrpc subgroup definition).

analyses.result_summary: Effect across PSA quartiles within clean subgroup: Q1 +63.3pp, Q2 +62.8pp, Q3 +62.4pp, Q4 +61.8pp. Adjusted enza:psa interaction β=-0.0002, p=0.94. The global negative enza:psa interaction reflects confounding with mcrpc/AR-V7/BRCA2/MSI; once these are conditioned on, PSA does not modify the enzalutamide effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the clean enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, ECOG, albumin and weight loss continue to be independent prognostic factors but do not abrogate the treatment effect.

analyses.code: logit('y ~ enza*psa + ecog + albumin + weight_loss', clean)
analyses.result_summary: Within clean subgroup: enza β=+3.02 (p<1e-300); ecog β=-0.405 (p=4e-38); albumin β=+0.168 (p=7e-5); weight_loss β=-0.045 (p=2e-16). These are general prognostic factors; they do NOT modify the enzalutamide benefit (no significant interactions). Effect persists across ECOG strata: ECOG 0 +62.8pp, ECOG 1 +60.2pp, ECOG 2 +57.0pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusted (logistic) tests confirm null effects of olaparib in BRCA2+, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high, and abiraterone in mcrpc=1.

analyses.code: for each (tx, target subgroup): logit('y ~ tx + adjustments') in target subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA2+ (n=4,996), adjusted for mcrpc/ecog/age/ar_v7: β=-0.271, p=0.053 (borderline negative). Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n=1,528): β=+0.018, p=0.95. Lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high (n=29,962): β=-0.003, p=0.94. Abiraterone in mCRPC (n=27,481): β=+0.004, p=0.92. Docetaxel overall: β=+0.002, p=0.94. None of the canonical biomarker-targeted pairings produces a positive treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic model restricted to mcrpc=0 with three biomarker-by-enzalutamide interactions cleanly captures the enzalutamide subgroup effect, with each biomarker individually β≈+3 for enzalutamide:negative-status.

analyses.result_summary: In mcrpc=0: baseline (all-modifier-negative) intercept = -1.58. enza main effect = +2.92 (p<1e-300). enza × ar_v7 = -2.68 (p=4e-192), enza × brca2 = -2.75 (p=2e-103), enza × msi_high = -2.64 (p=2e-37). Each modifier when present reverses the entire enzalutamide benefit. Predicted response (clean subgroup) ≈ σ(-1.58+2.92) ≈ 79%; (any modifier present, on enza) ≈ σ(-1.58+2.92-2.7) ≈ 21%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Enzalutamide: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive increase in objective_response (≈+62pp absolute, response 80% on enzalutamide vs 17% off enzalutamide) ONLY in the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this joint subgroup the effect is essentially null.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Olaparib: treatment_olaparib has no positive effect on objective_response in any biomarker-defined subgroup, including BRCA2-mutated patients (where canonically expected); the only borderline interaction is in the negative direction (olaparib slightly reduces response in BRCA2-mutated patients).

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Pembrolizumab: treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including MSI-high patients (where canonically expected).

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Lu177-PSMA: treatment_lu177_psma has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including PSMA-high mCRPC patients (where canonically expected).
FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Abiraterone: treatment_abiraterone has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including AR-V7-negative or mCRPC patients.
FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Docetaxel: treatment_docetaxel has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup tested.

analyses.result_summary: Clean subgroup n=15,681: enzalutamide on=79.8% (n=6,325) vs off=17.2% (n=9,356); absolute diff=+62.57pp; chi-square p<1e-300. Outside this joint subgroup, enzalutamide effect is essentially null (within-stratum diffs all between -6pp and +9pp).

analyses.code: logit in BRCA2+ subgroup, adjusted; full ITE search
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA2+ (n=4,996): adjusted β=-0.271 (p=0.053). Across the full ITE search no positive subgroup with stable lift was found (mean ITE ≈ 0).

analyses.code: logit in MSI-high subgroup, adjusted; full ITE search
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n=1,528): adjusted β=+0.018 (p=0.95). No subgroup with stable positive ITE found.

analyses.code: logit in PSMA-high subgroup, adjusted; full ITE search
analyses.result_summary: Lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high (n=29,962): adjusted β=-0.003 (p=0.94). No subgroup with stable positive ITE found.

analyses.code: logit in mCRPC subgroup adjusted by AR-V7; full ITE search
analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone in mCRPC (n=27,481): β=+0.004 (p=0.92). AR-V7- mCRPC: β=-0.030 (NS); AR-V7+ mCRPC: β=+0.16 (p=0.30). No subgroup with stable positive ITE found.

analyses.code: logit overall and stratified; full ITE search
analyses.result_summary: Docetaxel overall: β=+0.002 (p=0.94). No subgroup with stable positive ITE found.
